title: secure_zero word-aligned path assumes pointer alignment
description:
Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_util.h:40-51

The word-aligned write path casts volatile uint8_t* to volatile unsigned long* and writes. This assumes the pointer becomes aligned after the byte-by-byte prefix loop. The alignment check (uintptr_t)p & (sizeof(unsigned long) - 1) is correct, but the volatile cast sequence may cause issues:

1. The loop increments p (volatile uint8_t*) but then casts to (volatile unsigned long*)
2. If sizeof(unsigned long) differs between compile time and runtime (unlikely but possible with some toolchains), alignment could be wrong

What goes wrong: On architectures that require strict alignment (ARM Cortex-M without unaligned access), misaligned word writes would cause a HardFault. The alignment loop should prevent this, but the code path is subtle.

Recommendation: Add a compile-time assert that sizeof(unsigned long) is 4 or 8, matching common MCU word sizes.
